Cool Websites that I like and enjoy and use!

Back 2 Home - Games - Drawings - Videos - Music - Resources - Gunpla Zone - Crafts

Tools

🛈
An enigmatic color palette generator, harnessing the mystical witchcraft of polar coordinates! It's neat to have a color palette tool that isn't based on lame color science, but rather early-20th century esoterica.
Poline
🛈
Tools that do what they say on the box. They're quick and web-based, perfect for me.
NormalMap Online and TextureGenerator online
🛈
A tool for simulating economies, kind of like a diagram that you can run. Useful for balancing systems (if you want them to be balanced)!
Machinations.io
🛈
Quite simply a web-based way of using GraphViz. Very useful, mostly thanks to the immediate feedback.
GraphvizOnline
🛈
Web saver and remuxer. supports practically all media-containing websites that (un)intentionally make saving a hassle.
Cobalt.tools
🛈
Probably has the best video and audio saving programs available for free, basically a GUI for yt.dlp.
MediaHuman
🛈
is a nice reference for using HPGL pen plotters. Pen plotters are cool!!! There's also an article on my master's course's wiki that dives into them a bit, but I don't think I should link that :3c
The HP-GL Reference Guide
🛈
The classic cool text generator... Hell yeah!!!
cooltext.com
🛈
Has kind of become the webring script of choice for making a durable webring, I still need to try it for myself but I heard that it rocks.
Onionring.js
🛈
Useful for planning out home renovation stuff, currently using it to plan out a remodelling of our storage room.
Floor Plan Creator
🛈
Winamp in your browser, works great and is very cool!
Webamp
🛈
Instant 3D the HTML way! I love this stuff so much.
X3DOM
🛈
Google Sheet-based website maker, possibly useful for a lightweight solution of media-tracking.
Sheet Sites
🛈
My strange addiction is collecting .jpegs of moe-ified historical figures...
Fate/Grand Order Wiki
🛈
Free and unrestricted access to scientific papers, so far has had everything I wanted to look up. The web3/crypto shit is a bit ick though but not unsurprising given the political overlap.
sci-hub

Web Games and Toys

🛈
A Japanese HTML5 game developer or studio, not sure, my favourite of theirs is Fruit Box.
Game Saien
🛈
A free web ripoff of Suika Game, which is also a ripoff of a Chinese browser game. It came full circle and I think that's beautiful.
suikagame.com
🛈
A British indie game development studio. I've been playing their games since forever, probably since the site's inception. My favourite game from them is Skywire.
Nitrome
🛈
An audiovisual soundboard kind of thing. Works on desktop, but works better on a touchscreen!
Patatap
🛈
A web instrument, the parameters of which are saved in the URL, making sharing very easy. Really cool project!
Beepbox.co
🛈
A sort of multi-media browser-based visual novel...? All-time great narrative experience either way, go read it.
17776
🛈
Lets you play Yume Nikki and its many fangames in a sort of browser MMO way, it's basically Habbo Hotel.
The Yume Nikki Online Project
🛈
Aims to reproduce the Serial Experiments Lain PS2 game in web form; good game, good project, no notes.
Laingame.net
🛈
Converts pictures into pictures made out of character sets. ALso lets you paint with them.
Lvllvl
🛈
Roamable online exhibition type thing, very cool, very neat.
Entrance or Exit

Web People

🛈
Has a bunch of games, projects, drawings, writings, resources, tools; ton of cool stuff.
Everest Pipkin
🛈
Incredible projects such as the Antonymph Audiovisual Experience and CSS-Clicker, and some neat tools like Arrupted.
lyra.horse
🛈
Home of HALLEY LABS, great tune-craft resources and music.
heckscaper.com / em
🛈
Simply iconic and inspiring, violently personal just as it should be.
sabrin.party
🛈
Holy Fucking Bingle!!!
Maia Arson Crimew
🛈
Beautiful website full of heart.
Beau Jean
🛈
Extremely inspiring world of projects and other silliness!
Melonking

Assets

Textures

🛈
A wonderful Neocities website that hosts a ton of textures (3867 of them, as of writing) and even has a bunch of preview functionality!
TextureTown
🛈
A ton of hi-res photo textures, including architectural features, materials, nature elements, and backgrounds & landscapes. They're extremely well-categorized, and although maps and higher resolutions are paywalled, this is only 13 euros a year and they still have a ton of freebies. They also use '^-^' in their donation page so you know the money is in the right hands.
SketchUp Texture Club
🛈
A big variety of free textures free to use for personal and commercial purposes. I like to scale them down for PSX texturework.
Texturer
🛈
They have a big library of high quality, seamless PBR materials. Their pricing scheme is a bit evil though; when you subscribe you get an amount of credits per month, but unused credits don't stack and disappear when your sub ends.
textures.com
🛈
This internet archive item is, as far as I know, all that remains of GRSites' many resources. They also had stuff like fonts and neat tools like a logo maker, really sad that they shut down ;-;
GRSites Textures
🛈
A ton of free textures. Also some tutorials and tools but they're centered around Adobe products like Photoshop and After Effects.
Texturelabs
🛈
Blogspot page with a ton of photo textures, you can either manually download them or get a big pack with all of them prepacked for $14.99.
Seamless-Pixels

Audio

🛈
Has... uh... free sounds.
Freesound.org
🛈
Hosts a ton of modules and an active forum of trackers; They're great!
The Mod Archive
🛈
Has... free music... duhh.
Free Music Archive
🛈
Pretty good loops, samples, and synth presets.
Noiiz
🛈
A collection of free (game) sound effects created by the goat Taira Komori.
TK's Free Sound FX
🛈
All-time breakbeat bundles.
Rhythm Lab
🛈
No chuds, no meat and dairy promotion!
Mobygratis
🛈
Cool index site that contains manu many computer files pulled from archive.org, especially neat for music and SFX!
DiscMaster
🛈
Japanese royalty free background music and sound effects! Perfect for visual novels.
DOVA-SYNDROME

Images

🛈
Free stock photos and videos, I'm pretty sure this is where a lot of slop-producers get their random stock footage from, which is funny to me.
Pexels
🛈
Primarily deals in icons in a variety of filetypes, their other stuff can safely be ignored, it's a bit shit and may be AI generated.
ICONS8
🛈
Sells really cool fonts and illustrations. Sadly they, like textures.com, have a shitty subscription pricing scheme, but they do have a small selection of free stuff.
BLKMARKET
🛈
GIFCities bitch GIF GIF cities bitch (you can look up GIFs here that were scraped and preserved by the wayback machine).
GifCities
🛈
More of a -curated- collection of GIFs, moreso part of the web1.0 revival movement.
99GIF SHOP

Various

🛈
Has photo scan, 3D, and 2D assets of humans! A bunch of them are free, and they include things such as unwrapped face textures, (retopo'd) head and body scans, and reference photos.
3D.sk
🛈
A ton of free assets for game development, stuff like textures, models, sounds, fonts... Make sure to credit as is proper.
OpenGameArt.org
🛈
Basically a Creative Commons search engine that automatically directs you to material that has a CC license on different platforms, such as YouTube or Flickr.
The CC Search Portal
🛈
Royalty-free (vector) images, videos, music, sound effects, and GIFs.
Pixabay
🛈
A wiki/forum dedicated to creating and ripping video-game content, including sprites, models, textures, and sounds from all sorts of games and console eras. They're fun to use as placeholders.
The VG Resource

Tabletop

🛈
A website ran by a person known as Emily, who has hosted hundreds of name generators for over a decade. It has been an incredible resource for me, both for generating names and general inspiration.
Fantasy Name Generators
🛈
A dictionary of real-world names. It also features a lot of the history behind these names. I used it a lot for the naming in one of my settings which was heavily inspired by Finnish folklore.
Kate Monk's Onomastikon
🛈
A website I used for ages to write articles about my homebrewed-to-shit DnD 5e campaign. Reason I'm not using it anymore is mostly due to pricy subscription and a lack of running TTRPG games.
World Anvil
🛈
A website that aims to explain the alignment system in a human-parseable way, moderately impossible but I appreciate the effort and have used it in the past to introduce new players to this heavily vibes-based system.
The Alignment System
🛈
Features a ton of generators, the main one of which I used being their dungeon generators. There's clearly a lot of love behind this site and it has existed for ages. Buy them a coffee!
Donjon
🛈
A fantasy language generator. Very useful to create a basis for things like naming and individual words to sprinkle into roleplay when, for instance, a character doesn't know a certain word in 'common'.
Vulgarlang
🛈
Tools to procedurally generate spaces of varying scale. I especially got a lot of use out of the city generator.
Watabou's Procgen Arcana
🛈
An incredible tool for procedurally generating and designing coats of arms and other armorial bearings.
Armoria
🛈
Compiles hi-res card art made for the Magic: the Gathering TCG. I've often used these pieces for inspiration when trying to come up with characters, locations, and encounters.
Art of MTG

Webshops

🛈
A Dutch TTRPG seller, specializing in dice. They also sell handmade leather pouches!
Jack of Dice
🛈
A Dutch seller of everything chainmail, including materials, tools, instructions, etc.
The Queen Ring
🛈
SICK chokers, need one so bad...
Gutterscotch
🛈
Go-to for importing Japanese plastic.
Hobby Link Japan
🛈
Dutch weeb seller, they deliver to my local manga shop who I prefer to support tbh
Archonia
🛈
Instruments!!! And such other wonderful musical things!!!
Thomann
🛈
Game merch, I specifically love their plushies and such things.
IAm8Bit and FanGamer
🛈
Game merch, I specifically love their plushies and such things.
Grailed
🛈
German webshops are always cheaper and it's so fucked up.
Mindfactory.de
🛈
Dutch seller of boardgames and boardgame supplies, very useful for prototyping.
Spelspul
🛈
Belgian cosplay supply store, stuff like foam for making props and armor.
Cosplayshop
🛈
Leatherworking supply store in Den Bosch, awesome stuff but god it's so expensive.
Boomsma
🛈
They got damn near everything, very useful for some very specific recipes!
Tjin's International Foodstore
🛈
Western JFashion sellers, for cheaper options use a Bhiner/Taobao seller's services!
Devil Inspired and Glitzy Wonderland
🛈
Adorable jewelry, lotsa bling, extravagant and over the top. So cool!
Betsy Johnson
🛈
Beautiful platform sneakers, finally also in my size!
Anthony Wang
🛈
Really pretty fashion brand, their sustainability rating is quite mid though.
Minga London
🛈
My go-to source of books since Amazon bought and killed Book Depository. Still breaks my heart.
Better World Books

Neat Stuff

🛈
The render96 project, where they hunt down and present info on the texture and sound libraries Nintendo used in the creation of Super Mario 64's assets. Also a bunch of fun trivia!
Render96 Wiki and Render96 Archive
🛈
A folder for American homes from 1935, good layout inspo and generally a wonderful bit of history. Homes folders in general are super neat and a lot of them can be found on the internet archive.
Bawden Brothers Inc. Master Craft Homes
🛈
Combine Earthbound background layers to create neat GIFs!
Earthbound Battle Background JS
🛈
Tutorials by Jasper Flick, has been THE resource of learning Unity for me, and now also Godot. Written tutorials are great and there should be more of them.
Catlike Coding
🛈
Compiled sets of screengrabs and GIFs of game interfaces to be used as reference.
The Game UI Database and Interface In Game
🛈
A database of artworks. More browseable than Wikipedia, tightly categorized, and with some cool history about the pieces and artists. Good narrative inspo and fun to put in games as props.
Wikioo
🛈
Aims to map out gamedev organizations on a living map, it's kinda neat.
Gamedevmap
🛈
Lists cool data visualization projects, nicely categorized according to chart type.
Dataviz Inspiration
🛈
A sort of discovery-oriented search engine, currently supporting music, products, art, literature, and movies. It can give recommendations for them based on your likes, or you can browse a sort of web where similar artists and such are grouped together.
GNOD
🛈
Lets you look up words and sentences and spits out clips from movies and shows where that word or sentence is spoken. Fun for editing!
Yarn
🛈
Project by Xah Lee, lists all(?) unicode characters. Xah Lee is an interesting character and his website is an incredible feat in and of itself; the web equivalent of a massive dusty tome.
Unicode Index
🛈
An exhibit of old websites. My favourites include the Cartoon Network and 99rooms websites.
The Web Design Museum
🛈
A question I ask myself often...
what the fuck should i listen to right now.com
🛈
Lets you freely explore various game environments, good for inspo and reference.
Noclip
🛈
Experience the American TV and news cycle on and around the 11th of September, 2001. Made to be educational material, RFK Jr. could use it.
9/11 in Realtime
🛈
The most intriguing webring I've found so far.
The Apartment Webring
🛈
Computer museum of someone called Steve. Incredibly in-depth and interesting to scroll through.
Steve's Old Computer Museum
🛈
Fashion and makeup sustainability directory, rates and categorizes different brands. Wish they had a way to look up different -styles- of fashion but it works fine.
Good On You
(c) 2025 shoebby. All Rights Reserved.
